title: Phot.Venice.Basilica.La.Salute.041024.6645.jpg
tags: china plaza city travel venice india streets reflection berlin art bar night mall germany munich münchen square noche calle nikon asia strada place market nacht zurich hamburg chinese beijing ciudad places historic coolpix nightlife d200 rue venezia nuit venedig städte ville citta d300 strasen plätze colorphotoaward flickrstruereflection1
description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.

description: Italy, Venice, Carabinieri, 11.12 am, wondering where are the tourist & traders at the Riva degli Schiavoni, the usually lively, often overcrowded promenade along the waterfront between the Piazza di San Marco & Giardini pubblici, the public gardens  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. description: Italy, Venice, "Campo Bandiera & Moro o de la Bragora" with a peaceful atmosphere, the square is framed by the church of San Giovanni in Bragora & some beautiful buildings including the Palazzo Gritti-Badoer.  Siting on the benches for a break in the shade of the trees in the square, you can observe the "original fake merchandise" street vendors who are meeting or passing by here. However, while I was sitting here, I did not see once a vendor approaching or harassing anyone on the marketplace.  The origin of the term Bragora is unclear, but relates to the church "San Giovanni in Bragora", located on the square & founded in the early 8th century, Pietro Barbo, 1417-1471, future Pope Paul II & Antonio Vivaldi, 1678 -1741 were baptised in the church.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, the “Caffè Florian” under the arcades of the “Procuratie Nuove” at the San Marco Square was founded by the Cafétier Floriano Francesconi in December 1720 under the name "Venezia Trionfante", Triumphant Venice, but the Venetian only called the Café by the first name of the owner & so it was changed later into “Caffè Florian”.   The Café used to be the place to meet for Italy’s patriots, artists & intellectuals as well as international well-known personalities; today’s patrons are basically tourists.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, the “Caffè Florian” under the arcades of the “Procuratie Nuove” at the San Marco Square was founded by the Cafétier Floriano Francesconi in December 1720 under the name "Venezia Trionfante", Triumphant Venice, but the Venetian only called the Café by the first name of the owner & so it was changed later into “Caffè Florian”.   The Café used to be the place to meet for Italy’s patriots, artists & intellectuals as well as international well-known personalities; today’s patrons are basically tourists.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the venetian buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, without their unique buildings & their patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canale Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice,…I think.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, water taxi at the Puente Rialto.   ⦂⚪(‘..,Unfortunately I had not a good experience with a water taxi.  I booked my return trip to the airport with the water taxi via my longstanding, serious travel agency. The same price for the trip, € 95 for one or two person, so far so good. Leaving from the Piazza di San Marco, guests were then picked up from other hotels, which took a good 30 minutes until all 10 seats were taken & until the taxi driver took the trip to the airport, each customer paid the same price.  📌...a little tip,  1. if you take a water taxi, then go early & do not go the last minute if you dont want miss your flight back home,  2. with the water bus, the" Vaporetto", it works as well for just around € 7. Because the taxi, besides you, they collect more people until the taxi is full - total 10 pax - only than they hit the “road” to the airport.  I was the first in the taxi & pre-paid with luggade € 90 one way when i booked the trip, Until they had collected all the other people from the hotels, about 30 minutes had passed.  ......However, I had a great time in Venice.  👍...However, the lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Harry's.Bar.Bellini.01.041025.5263.jpg
tags: nikon d300 d200 coolpix beijing china chinese city art nightlife night nacht noche nuit travel square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic reflection mall bar market städte ciudad ville citta colorphotoaward india asia germany hamburg munich münchen berlin zurich venice venedig venezia d800
description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,… however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,… well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. Just to mention a few like Charlie Chaplin, Princess Aspasia of Greece, Alfred Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, Truman Capote, Arturo Toscanini, Orson Welles, Baron Philippe de Rothschild, Aristotle Onassis, Barbara Hutton, Peggy Guggenheim, Woody Allen, George Cloone, & today mainly by simple tourists like me.  My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Without its past heritage it would be just another simple corner bar without any special flair & a service attitude like, "just another tourist-voyeur we will not see again", seems they don’t care about a good tip for adequate service.  Anyway, ... I was there, I saw & felt the "atmosphere", it was not good, it was not bad,....it was just the "opposite", ⦂⚪)… cheers…🍸,  ... like "Alfredos" restaurant in Rome, the creator of "Fettuccine Alfredo", I came, I saw & I'm enriched now for one more "one-time experience" & that's enough, "Veni, Vidi, Effugit",  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Venice’s only female Gondoliera, Alexandra Hai from Germany, known as Alex & as the rebel of the 21 century in Venice.
